pattern: Precomputing each node's depth and subtree height, plus the best height achievable at each depth excluding any single node, lets every removal query be answered in constant time.
hints: Removing a subtree only changes the answer through the longest root-to-leaf path that passed through it, so think about depths.
For a removed node at some depth, the surviving height is the deepest path that avoids that node, which depends on the other nodes at the same depth.

Precompute each node's depth and subtree height; then for every depth keep the top two reach values (depth + subtree height) so a removed node's answer is the best reach at its depth that is not its own.

edge_inputs.description: single query on an asymmetric tree
several queries on a balanced tree
two-node tree, remove the only child
removing a leaf off the longest path does not shrink height
removing the node on the unique deepest path

stress.description: random near-complete tree with many queries hitting varied nodes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    vals = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(vals)
    # build a random-shaped tree via a list-array but allow gaps to vary shape
    # simplest valid: complete tree array form gives a dense tree of n nodes
    tree = vals
    m = rng.randint(1, min(n - 1, 500))
    # query values are node values != root (root is tree[0])
    pool = [v for v in vals if v != tree[0]]
    queries = [rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(m)]
    return [tree, queries]
stress.note: dense array form is a valid complete-tree level-order; queries avoid the root value
